sources: The Coca-Cola Company 2026 CAGNY Investor Presentation
Q4 2025 Earnings Call Guidance (Henrique Braun, CEO)

2026.methodology: Income forecast is based on the mid-point of management's 4.5% organic revenue growth guidance applied to 2025 actuals ($47.941B * 1.0455 ≈ $50.125B). Spending is calculated by subtracting the projected Net Income of $14.0B (derived from the targeted 8% growth in comparable EPS and stable margins) from Total Income. The breakdown reflects a continued focus on margin expansion through the 'bottling system transformation' and refranchising efforts, leading to a slight decrease in the 'Finished Product' income share and optimized SG&A spending.

sources: Q4 and Full Year 2025 Earnings Release (February 10, 2026)

2025.methodology: Total Income is the reported Net Operating Revenue of $47.941B. Total Spending ($37.228B) is calculated as Net Revenue minus Net Income ($10.713B). Category percentages are derived from the 2025 Consolidated Statements of Income: COGS was $18.397B, SG&A was $14.522B, Interest Expense was $1.654B, and Income Taxes were $2.861B. Other Operating Charges were significantly lower in 2025 compared to 2024 due to the absence of prior year's large restructuring hits.

sources: SEC Form 10-K Consolidated Statements of Income

2024.methodology: Data extracted from the FY2024 financial results. Total Income represents Net Operating Revenues ($47.1B). Total Spending calculated as Net Revenue minus Net Income ($10.631B + $0.639B non-controlling interests). Breakdown percentages are derived from the consolidated income statement: COGS was $18.75B, SG&A was $14.2B, and other charges including Fairlife/Bodyarmor adjustments totaled approximately $4.16B.
